question_text: A microwave oven has a power rating of 900 W and a kettle has a power rating of 2.4 kW. Both are switched on at the same time for 5 minutes. Calculate the total energy transferred by both appliances and explain which one transfers more energy.
correct_answer: Microwave: 270,000 J (or 270 kJ); Kettle: 720,000 J (or 720 kJ). The kettle transfers more energy because it has a higher power rating.
explanation: Convert 5 minutes to 300 seconds, then use E = P × t. For the microwave: 900 × 300 = 270,000 J. For the kettle: 2,400 × 300 = 720,000 J. The kettle transfers more energy because power and energy are directly proportional when time is the same.
